reviews.review_description: I've spent a couple days trying to figure out how to get this to work because one of my friends said that I don't need a very strong computer to use alvr (my computer isn't very strong at all) so I decided I should try and I've genuinely searched over so many tutorials and everything and not a single one has actually helped like this is actually worse than brawl stars bru 🫩

I either leave it on and have abysmal stuttering/framepacing issues or leave it off and it saturates my encoder and I have to turn down quality to 30 mbps and still have some frame pacing issues due to it not being in sync even though I have very good preformance headroom.

Before i start i should mention i use Linux mint. Anyway, i wanted to play vr but steam vr keeps giving me error 3003 even though i tried reinstalling steam VR four times. Alvr used to work but now it doesn't.
Like bro i tried everything it worked before now it doens like fix it i cant play gtag  on  my bad laptop😡😡😡😡😡😡😡😡
Thanks to ALVR, I got games like HL2VR, Boneworks and Vertigo 2 running on my Steam Deck! Love it, no issues.
actually so good, my trash pc can basically run anything because of this love it sm

Setting it up was pretty simple. It requires installing the ALVR stream app on your desktop computer, which was relatively simple. Pairing the desktop to the headset was nearly automatic too, just had to accept the link in the desktop app. Once installed, the app works pretty well, although not perfectly. Even with the codec at 30Mbps I had some few moments in which I lost image for around a second. It happened just 3 times in around 1 hour, so for me is not that critical.

I installed the desktop streamer app in Kubuntu 25.10 without any problem, following the setup wizard in the app.
im on the pc app and on the quest app and it is not finding my headset

reviews.review_title: A HUGE thanks for Linux compatibility!
reviews.review_description:
I love ALVR for the Linux support! I just had to read the setup instructions and I was up and running in all of about 5 minutes. I love the (nearly) endless possibilites in configuration and tweaking and I really like the look and feel of using ALVR. However, if I have to take my Headset off for a moment and it goes to stand-by, it does not resume seamlessly. There are many, many connection losses an automatic reconnect attempts until it decides to somewhat work again but in reality I have to restart SteamVR/ALVR to reconnect cleanly. But when it works, it works like a charm!
I really would like to see a kind of profile management option like profile 1 is aimed at lower graphics and higher refresh rate and profile 2 is the other way around so I don't need to dial in all the settings manually everytime.
Keep up the good work!
It's the only way I've been able to play pcvr since I switched to an arc b580 GPU. The settings need to be tweaked but it runs great when the headset is hard wired to my PC.
Its amazing connects fast and good if u lag turn down ur mbps and for it to be more pixelated turn it up

reviews.review_title: The reason I was able to switch to Linux
reviews.review_description: This app is awesome. VR was one of the few things stopping me from ditching Windows. I heard vr was possible with ALVR but it was really complicated. That's old news though! I was up and running playing my favorite VR titles immediately. Most important was Automobilista 2 with my wheel and pedals. I just had to add an argument into Steam VR so everything would show up. A lot of great settings to tune to get the best image and frame rate. I'm running this flawlessly on CachyOS! I downloaded WlxOverlay so I can see my desktop. 😃
what default settings so bad but still it’s super good if you get the right settings

reviews.review_title: Finally i can play PC VR.
reviews.review_description: Because my GPU doesn't have hardware encoding i couldn't play PC VR via steam or oculus. But recently i discovered ALVR and finally i don't have to play standalone on my quest 2 anymore. The setup was very easy. İ've  had my problems at the beginning with a black screen and bad connections but i fixed it all with some tweaks in the settings. İ would recommend anyone who has a USB WiFi dongle to upgrade on a pcie wifi card, because doing so increases the image quality drastically.

I have incredibly good router. Compared to VD its incredibly bad. Tried to tweak everything none of those worked. I also used 5 Gbps Ethernet cable. 
Just terrible.

reviews.review_title: Lets me play SteamVR games on Linux!
reviews.review_description: This made it possible to play most of my SteamVR games like Half-Life: Alyx on Linux!

far superior to meta link, and the wireless mode works just as well too. it has plenty of options.

Oculus failed me the last time so I switched. The foveating compression makes it look much crisper and less jpeg like for a given bitrate. The presets work well and you can go deeper to fiddle if you want.

Get desktop+ on steam to make this a better experience.

reviews.review_title: I’ve Been so good on gtag cause of alvr
reviews.review_description: The preds are PERFECT even though it’s laggy pretty good app and I love the way I can connect multiple headsets very good 10/10
its good but the lag needs to be fixed

This was the best supported Intel Arc GPu workaround that I found. At least the only one that was free and still pretty useful. I hear Virtual Desktop is best but I can shell out the money for it after finally building my first PC. Every game I’ve tried to PCVR stream with it has worked great! Just make sure you follow the set up and take some time to fiddle with the settings. I set my constant Bitrate to about 250 and it’s been pretty smooth on my Intel Arc B580.

It’d be great if it was able to help optimize the performance though. So many options it gets confusing.

reviews.review_title: Works Perfect to Bring VR to Linux
reviews.review_description: I was actually surprised at how easy it was to get VR running with Linux. After installing the application on your computer and doing some fine tuning of the settings for your set up, it just works. Kudos to the ALVR team for making VR on Linux basically seem native!
ALVR is the best for steam better then steam airlink and runs smoother I I could give it a 100 star rating I would ALVR ON TOP
Mine is saying error 11

I don't know why it's taken me so long to write a review for ALVR as I have been using it for probably about two years now.  Admittedly it hasn't been officially available on the Meta store that long, but it's been here for quite a while now.

Before I found ALVR I was limited to the offerings on the Quest headset.  There's nothing really wrong with that as the catalogue is increasing fairly quickly, but I had a number of PCVR titles I could never play as Meta does not support Linux with their software (strange as their headsets run Android which is just a custom Linux distribution) and Steam also does not currently support PC link with their software.

With ALVR I can now play titles like Half-Life (2 with the VR mod, and Alyx), Star Wars: Squadrons, Elite Dangerous, Assetto Corsa Competizione, as well as a number of other titles.

This software has gotten better with each release.  Long may it continue!
DEVELOPERS ARE AWESOME!

Working great on Slackware with a wired setup. I just had to switch from PulseAudio to PipeWire to get audio working. Make sure you read the troubleshooting guides if you have trouble. Thanks developers!

This app does everything you could want it to, including functioning on Linux (some might say even better than it does on Windows!) There's really nothing else to say, it's so good that you don't notice it.
